question: Test Your Knowledge: What is a converter dolly?
choices: A safety device that prevents trailer skids
A coupling device with one or two axles and a fifth wheel used to couple a semitrailer to the rear of a tractor-trailer combination
A hydraulic brake system component
A device used to measure trailer air pressure
explanation: A converter gear on a dolly is a coupling device of one or two axles and a fifth wheel by which a semitrailer can be coupled to the rear of a tractor-trailer combination forming a double bottom rig.

question: Test Your Knowledge: Do converter dollies typically have spring brakes?
choices: Yes, all converter dollies have spring brakes
No, they never have spring brakes
Some do and some don't, depending on the manufacturer
Spring brakes are only on the tractor unit
explanation: Some second trailers don't have spring brakes, which is why special procedures are needed to secure them before coupling by charging and then disconnecting the emergency line.

question: Test Your Knowledge: What are the methods to secure a second trailer before coupling?
choices: Only use wheel chocks
Drive close, connect emergency line, charge air tank, disconnect emergency line; or use wheel chocks; or use spring brakes
Only manual locking devices can be used
Trailers are always pre-secured by the facility
explanation: If the second trailer doesn't have spring brakes, you can drive the tractor close, connect the emergency line, charge the trailer air tank, and disconnect it to set brakes; alternatively, you can use wheel chocks or rely on spring brakes if equipped.

question: Test Your Knowledge: How should trailer height be set before backing a converter dolly under the rear trailer?
choices: Level with the center of the fifth wheel
Slightly higher than the center of the fifth wheel
Slightly lower than the center of the fifth wheel
As high as possible to clear the dolly tongue
explanation: Trailer height must be slightly lower than the center of the fifth wheel so the trailer is raised slightly when the dolly is pushed under it.

question: Test Your Knowledge: What do you check when making a visual check of the coupling?
choices: Only that the dolly is attached
That the landing gear is lowered
That there is no space between upper and lower fifth wheel and that locking jaws are closed on the kingpin
That all lights are working
explanation: A visual check of coupling should confirm no space between the upper and lower fifth wheel and that the locking jaws are closed on the kingpin.

question: Test Your Knowledge: Why should you pull a dolly out from under a trailer before disconnecting it from the trailer in front?
choices: To reduce wear on the fifth wheel
To prevent the dolly tow bar from flying up and causing injury
To make it easier to inspect the kingpin
To reduce air pressure in the lines
explanation: You should never unlock the pintle hook while the dolly is still under the rear trailer because the dolly tow bar may fly up, possibly causing injury and making it very difficult to re-couple.

question: Test Your Knowledge: What critical items should you check when inspecting the converter dolly and pintle hook?
choices: Only that the dolly has air brakes
The fifth wheel condition, locking mechanisms, and the pintle hook latching and security
Just the tire tread depth
The color and condition of paint
explanation: When inspecting converter dolly and pintle hook, check the sliding fifth wheel condition, all locking pins and latches, the pintle-eye placement in the hook, and that the hook is properly latched and safety chains are secured.

question: Test Your Knowledge: Should the shut-off valves on the rear of the last trailer in a set of doubles be open or closed?
choices: Open at all times
Closed
Open during charging, closed during operation
It depends on the manufacturer
explanation: Shut-off valves at the rear of the last trailer should be closed, while shut-off valves at the rear of the front trailer should be open.

question: Test Your Knowledge: How do you test that air flows to all trailers in a doubles or triples combination?
choices: Check the tractor air gauge only
Push the air supply knob in, then open the emergency line shut-off valve at the rear of the last trailer and listen for air escaping
Look at the trailer light indicators
Ask another driver to confirm
explanation: To test that air flows to all trailers, push in the red air supply knob, go to the rear of the rig, and open the emergency line shut-off valve at the rear of the last trailer; you should hear air escaping, showing the entire system is charged.

question: Test Your Knowledge: How can you identify if your converter dolly is equipped with antilock brakes?
choices: Check the owner's manual only
Listen for an antilock braking sound during braking
Look for a yellow lamp on the left side of the dolly
Check if the dolly is painted yellow
explanation: Converter dollies built on or after March 1, 1998, are required to have antilock brakes and will have a yellow lamp on the left side of the dolly.
